rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Trustees Motion To Compel Compliance And For Order To Show Cause Why Respondent Mark Borsuk Should Not Be Held In Contempt
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michel McNaughton. Before the court is Temporary Trustee's Motion to Compel Compliance and for Order to Show Cause Why Respondent Mark Borsuk Should Not Be Held in Contempt ("Motion"). No timely opposition to the Motion was filed. Per the Notice of Motion, movants seeks an order pursuant to CCP sec. 1209 requiring suspended trustee Mark Borsuk to show cause why he should not be held in contempt, fined $1,000 and ordered to pay the attorneys' fees and costs of bringing the motion, for not complying with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee. Per the Notice of Motion, movants also seek an order pursuant to CCP sec. 128, compelling Mr. Borsuk to turn over all Trust property in accordance with the June 3, 2024 order, including the key to the padlock Mr. Borsuk allegedly put on the storage unit holding Trust records, and all Trust records stored at his house or in his home office. The Motion is GRANTED. Mark Borsuk is ordered to immediately turn over all Trust property in his possession or control to temporary trustee David A. Bradlow, including all keys to the padlock Mr. Borsuk allegedly put on the storage unit holding Trust records, and all Trust records allegedly stored at Mr. Borsuk's home or in his home office, in accordance with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee. The court will separately issue and set for hearing Order to Show Cause Why Mark Borsuk Should Not Be Held in Contempt for Failure to Comply with Court Order, ordering Mr. Borsuk to show cause why he should not be held in contempt for failing to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee, and fined $1,000 and ordered to pay the reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred by this party in connection with the contempt proceeding, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sec. 1218(a) (see also, Moore v. Sup.Ct. (Barsky) (2020) 57 CA5th 441, 463-465). Hearing on the Order the Show Cause will be set for April 30, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. in Department 204 of this court, located at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, California. Any written response to the Order to Show Cause shall be filed no later than April 13, 2026. Movants shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
